author: @fionahite170
text: If you have enough free 3D printers can we please get onto something else

author: @stewartpalmer2456
text: I will admit, having one of these is on my future list. But I'd also point out that I've used a serious amount of the washable resins and there is always a drying time after water washing. The water doesn't just evaporate like alcohol. Also not always do you get a clean wash. There are times I get a scum left on the print which has to be wiped off. So, it's six in one hand and a half dozen in the other.

author: @marjon1703
text: So it's big enough for Mars and Saturn printers, but is it big enough for Uranus? {Giggle}

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - well i suppose you could try if you needed it lol

author: @ericheft6184
text: What I'm more interested in is the pro/con's of resin printing over the main stream filament based prints. My understanding ( which may be incorrect ) is resin printing generally has the ability to create finer details and stronger parts at the cost of post processing. Both require good ventilation because of the off gassing of the materials, unless you just do PLA. Do you have some ideas on what you would need resin printing for that you couldn't get with FDM using some of the more expensive filament types?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: next video mate - i tend to do these as a kind of series to cover everything - if I don't I can guarantee folks saying why didn't you do such and such - but then if I do I can guarantee folks saying why why haven't you done such and such - for me it's no win lol

author: @TheEudaemonicPlague
text:
I'm sure this is all interesting to people doing 3D printing, but couldn't you at least give those of us not involved a clue, so we don't find ourselves watching something of absolutely no interest? Just some visible clue that the topic is 3D printing.....

BTW, you drive me nuts, looking so much like Bill Owen....heh. I wish he was still around, but no one lives forever. Last of the Summer Wine, was where I became familiar with him.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: generally the clue is in the title mate

author: @kevinleebailey
text: I'm wondering if this machine could be used for curing and cleaning silk screens. If so Elegoo could market this device to silk screen printers. Obviously a larger version for large screens could be developed.

author: @ashercorbett8089
text:
Great stuff! You might be interested in this device based on Tesla’s “Apparatus for the utilization of radiant energy” patent:

It’s a PDF called “Aether Tunnels to Produce Free Energy for Free Electricity” by Richard Watkins. (Can’t post link)

I think it’d be fantastic if you could build and prove if it’s rubbish or not! Seems like it could have merit 🎉

author: @xrok1
text: I love your channel. Please dont become a review channel. People come to you for practical hands on projects. Love your enthusiasm. Keep it up

replies.author: @Ammoniummetavanadate
replies.text: He probably had an agreement to get the reviews out by the end of the month and was behind schedule.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Mate - I get asked to do reviews by folks who watch the channel - also  in part it's how I fund the channel. But, I wouldn't worry - I hardly do any reviews as you know but i get about 10 or 15 review requests every day!
